name: MiMo-V2.5-Pro
archDisplay: Sparse MoE (384 routed experts, 8/token), 70 layers (1 dense + 69 MoE), hybrid Sliding-Window+Global attention 6:1 with learnable attention-sink bias, 3x MTP modules; text-only (no native vision/audio encoders)
note: Xiaomi's flagship coding/agentic model, 1.02T total / 42B active MoE, 1M context, fully open-sourced under MIT. Optimized for very long agentic trajectories (1,000+ tool calls); unlike sibling MiMo-V2.5, it is text-only (no native image/video/audio encoders).

results.notes: SWE-bench Verified; from the embedded fbCategories chart data on Xiaomi's official MiMo-V2.5-Pro product page. Independently corroborated (same 78.9 figure) via automatio.ai.
SWE-Bench Pro; from the embedded fbCategories/benchData chart arrays (consistent value in both blocks on the page).
Terminal-Bench 2.0; from the embedded benchmark chart data.
Humanity's Last Exam, with tool use (the page also reports 34.0 'w.o. tools'); from the embedded fbCategories chart data.

GDPVal-AA (labeled 'Elo' on the page, but the taxonomy's own unit for this slug is pts on a 0-2000 scale, which matches this value) from the 'General Agent' category of the embedded fbCategories chart data on Xiaomi's official MiMo-V2.5-Pro product page; MiMo-V2.5-Pro scores 1581 vs Claude Opus 4.6 1606, Gemini 3.1 Pro 1317, GPT-5.4 1674, Kimi K2.6 1480, GLM 5.1 1535, DeepSeek V4 Pro 1554, and the prior-gen MiMo-V2-Pro 1426. [score corrected during round-5 full-corpus accuracy re-verify: 1266 -> 1581]

GPQA Diamond, independently measured by Artificial Analysis. Not shown in AA's rendered (JS-hydrated) page text, but present as a raw field ('gpqa': 0.865656565656566) inside the model's embedded Next.js data payload fetched directly from the live page HTML. Corroborated verbatim by a third-party mirror, designforonline.com's MiMo-V2.5-Pro profile ('GPQA Diamond: 86.6%'). Not previously in the corpus.

IFBench (instruction-following), independently measured by Artificial Analysis (raw 'ifbench': 0.798639455782313 in the model's embedded JSON payload). Corroborated verbatim by designforonline.com's mirror ('IFBench: 79.9%'). New benchmark, not previously in the corpus for this model.

τ²-Bench Telecom, independently measured by Artificial Analysis (raw field 'tau2': 0.941520467836257 in the model's embedded JSON payload). AA's dedicated public leaderboard confirms this metric key specifically denotes '𝜏²-Bench Telecom' (https://artificialanalysis.ai/evaluations/tau2-bench), distinct from AA's newer 'τ³-Banking' evaluation which this same model reports separately (field 'tauBanking': 0.0866, no matching taxonomy slug, excluded). Corroborated by designforonline.com's identical mirror figure ('τ²-Bench: 94.2%'). Moderate-high confidence: the field is present and consistent across two sources, though AA's current UI surfaces τ³-Banking rather than τ²-Telecom for this model, suggesting the underlying eval may be a carried-over/legacy measurement rather than a freshly re-run one.

LiveCodeBench, independently measured by Vals AI. Verified via rendered screenshot ('81.35%'), since raw fetched HTML text shows a placeholder '0.0%' before the client-side animation completes; cross-checked against the CSS bar's 'target-width:81.354%' attribute in the raw HTML, which matches. New benchmark, not previously in the corpus for this model. Note: a separate, unrelated third-party aggregator (automatio.ai, echoed by SEO-content sites) claims a similar-looking 80.6% LiveCodeBench figure for this model, but the existing corpus file already flags that aggregator's whole benchmark table as likely-fabricated/unreliable -- this Vals AI figure is sourced independently from that flagged table and is not a reuse of it.

MMLU-Pro, independently measured by Vals AI. Verified via rendered screenshot ('84.59%'); raw HTML CSS bar 'target-width:84.595%' matches. New benchmark, not previously in the corpus for this model (the HF README's MMLU-Pro figure of 68.5 is explicitly for the pretrained Base checkpoint, not this released chat model, and was correctly excluded already).

SWE-bench Verified, independently re-measured by Vals AI using their own bash-tool-only mini-SWE-agent harness (Tier-2 independent evaluator per Vals AI's own methodology) -- distinct from Xiaomi's self-reported 78.9% already in this file. A harder/lower, less-favorable independent data point included deliberately for thoroughness. Verified via rendered screenshot ('74.00%'); raw HTML CSS bar 'target-width' matches.

Terminal-Bench 2.1, independently re-measured by Vals AI (own harness) -- distinct from Xiaomi's self-reported 68.4% already in this file. A notably harder/lower, less-favorable independent data point included deliberately for thoroughness rather than cherry-picking only favorable figures. Verified via rendered screenshot ('57.30%'); raw HTML CSS bar 'target-width:57.303%' matches.

Humanity's Last Exam, independently measured by Artificial Analysis without tool access (raw 'hle': 0.337812789620019 in the model's embedded JSON payload) -- distinct from the tool-augmented 48.0% already in this file's results. Closely matches Xiaomi's own self-reported 'w.o. tools' figure of 34.0% shown in the same chart data that produced the existing 48.0 entry, but that no-tools variant was never itself recorded as a separate result until now. A harder (no-tools) data point, included for thoroughness.

Official PinchBench (OpenClaw agent benchmark) leaderboard average score for MiMo-V2.5-Pro specifically (best score 89.5%), confirmed directly on PinchBench's own site via live fetch. Genuinely new: only the sibling MiMo-V2.5 (non-Pro) model currently has a pinchbench entry in this corpus (89.7); this is the first pinchbench entry for the Pro variant, and the two scores are close but distinct, consistent with them being separately-run evaluations of different models.

Text Arena (Overall) Elo on arena.ai, confirmed directly via live fetch of the 'mimo-v2.5-pro' row: rank 32, Elo 1466, +/-5 CI, 39,035 votes, MIT license, $0.43/$0.87 pricing, 1M context -- all matching this exact model. This figure was already referenced inside the sibling MiMo-V2.5 (non-Pro) file's own verificationNotes ('explicitly distinct on the same leaderboard from mimo-v2.5-pro at 1466') but was never itself recorded as a result row in the Pro model's own file until now -- genuinely missing entry, not a new discovery of the number itself.

Artificial Analysis's τ³-Banking evaluation (raw field 'tauBanking': 0.0866 in the model's embedded JSON payload), distinct from the tracked τ²-Bench Telecom row. [taxonomy-gap backfill: this score was already verified in an earlier research round but had no matching benchmark slug at the time; added once the slug was introduced.]

SealedBench sealed capability index — composite of capability, token efficiency, speed and cost; rank 18/28 on the 2026-07-22 run (sealedbench.com). Listed as "MiMo-2.5-Pro" (Xiaomi). Evaluated at the highest available thinking setting. SealedBench 'MiMo-2.5-Pro' is this corpus's 'MiMo-V2.5-Pro'.

verificationNotes:
Params/architecture/context/license confirmed directly against the raw HuggingFace README (XiaomiMiMo/MiMo-V2.5-Pro). SWE-bench Verified score (78.9) independently cross-confirmed identical across three sources: Xiaomi's own product-page benchmark chart, a WebSearch snippet from automatio.ai, and a separate search-result summary — high confidence. IMPORTANT CAVEAT: the numeric benchmark table inside the HuggingFace README (GSM8K 99.6, GPQA-Diamond 66.7, MMLU 89.4, etc.) is explicitly labeled 'Base Model Evaluation' — i.e. it scores the pretrained MiMo-V2.5-Pro-Base checkpoint, not the released/post-trained chat model this entry represents — so those numbers were deliberately NOT used in 'results' below to avoid misrepresenting the shipped model. Instead, results are taken from the live JS-driven comparison charts embedded in Xiaomi's own MiMo-V2.5-Pro product page, fetched and parsed directly from raw HTML/JS (bypassing any summarization risk), which score the actual released model against Claude Opus 4.6, Gemini 3.1 Pro, GPT-5.4, Kimi K2.6, GLM 5.1, DeepSeek V4 Pro. A third-party aggregator (automatio.ai) also lists a much wider benchmark table (GPQA 54%, MMLU 86.7%, LiveCodeBench 80.6%, and even MMMU scores despite Pro being text-only) that conflicts substantially with both the vendor's own base-model table and the vendor's chat-model chart data, and separately implies multimodality Xiaomi itself denies for this model — treated as unreliable/likely-fabricated and excluded entirely.

[2026-07-16 core-benchmark gap sweep for mmlu/math/aime/humaneval/mmmu]: Checked all three source tiers -- (1) Xiaomi's own launch materials: the HF README (XiaomiMiMo/MiMo-V2.5-Pro) contains MMLU 89.4, MMLU-Redux 92.8, MMLU-Pro 68.5, GSM8K 99.6, MATH 86.2, AIME 24&25 37.3, and HumanEval+ 75.6, but every one of these lives exclusively under the README's '### Base Model Evaluation' table (confirmed via raw README fetch), i.e. they score the pretrained MiMo-V2.5-Pro-Base checkpoint, not the released/post-trained chat model this file represents -- there is no separate post-trained/chat evaluation table with these benchmarks anywhere in the README (only 'Long-context Evaluation' follows), and Xiaomi's own product page (mimo.xiaomi.com/mimo-v2-5-pro/) confirmed via raw-HTML parse of both its fbCategories and benchData chart arrays contains none of MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval/MMMU for the shipped model -- only GDPVal-AA, τ³-bench, Claw-Eval, HLE, SWE-Bench Pro/Verified, MiMo Coding Bench, FrontierSWE, and Terminal-Bench 2.0. (2) Independent leaderboards: Vals.ai's MiMo-V2.5-Pro page (raw HTML target-width bars) has only MMLU Pro (already tracked) with no separate MMLU, MATH, AIME, HumanEval, or MMMU rows; Artificial Analysis's page (raw JSON payload, 3.2MB fetch) has zero fields matching mmlu/math/aime/humaneval/mmmu anywhere -- its Intelligence Index for this model is explicitly composed of GDPval-AA v2, τ³-Banking, Terminal-Bench v2.1, SciCode, HLE, GPQA Diamond, CritPt, AA-Omniscience, and AA-LCR, none of which are the target benchmarks; OpenRouter's benchmarks tab likewise has no matches. designforonline.com (a mirror that has faithfully echoed AA's GPQA/IFBench/τ²-Bench figures elsewhere in this file) also has none of these five benchmarks for this model. (3) Sibling cross-reference: the sibling MiMo-V2.5 (non-Pro) file independently corroborates the pattern -- it also has no mmlu, math, aime, or humaneval entries (only mmlu-pro and mmmu, the latter because that sibling is natively multimodal, unlike Pro). One low-quality aggregator, automatio.ai, does list MMLU 86.7%, MATH, AIME 2025, HumanEval, and MMMU/MMMU-Pro figures for this model, but that exact source is already flagged elsewhere in this file's verificationNotes as likely-fabricated -- its own MMMU claim for a model Xiaomi explicitly documents as text-only (no vision encoder) is direct evidence of that fabrication, so it was not used. Conclusion: mmlu, math, aime, and humaneval are legitimate absences -- Xiaomi has evidently retired these near-saturated legacy benchmarks in favor of harder suites (GPQA-Diamond, HLE, LiveCodeBench, MMLU-Pro, agentic/coding evals) for its post-trained chat models, consistent with the frontier-lab trend; mmmu is inapplicable by architecture, since MiMo-V2.5-Pro is confirmed text-only with no image/video/audio encoders (per its own spec sources and note field). All five gaps resolved as omitted_no_evidence; no existing result rows were altered.

[2026-07-17 second-pass deep sweep for mmlu/math/aime/humaneval, per instructions to check ONLY source types not already exhausted by the 2026-07-16 sweep (official launch materials, HF README, Vals.ai, Artificial Analysis, OpenRouter, designforonline.com, sibling-file cross-reference, automatio.ai were all already checked]: Systematically checked all 6 newly-specified source categories.

(1) Stanford HELM: Fetched crfm.stanford.edu/helm/classic/latest/, /helm/lite/latest/, and /helm/capabilities/latest/ directly -- page shells returned without a queryable model list via WebFetch, so cross-checked definitively via HELM's own GitHub source: stanford-crfm/helm's src/helm/config/model_deployments.yaml (the canonical registry of every model HELM is capable of evaluating) contains zero entries for "Xiaomi," "MiMo," or "mimo" in any casing -- meaning HELM has never registered this model family for evaluation at all, let alone run MMLU against it. Also discovered (via search) that HELM entered maintenance mode on 2026-06-01, shortly after MiMo-V2.5-Pro's 2026-04-22 release, making it very unlikely the model was ever queued before evaluation activity wound down. Strong negative evidence.

(2) arXiv / Semantic Scholar: Searched "MiMo-V2.5-Pro" site:arxiv.org, "MiMo-V2.5-Pro MMLU", and Semantic-Scholar-style baseline queries. No dedicated arXiv technical report exists for MiMo-V2.5-Pro as of this search. Fetched the one plausible sibling/family paper that did surface, "MiMo-V2-Flash Technical Report" (arxiv.org/abs/2601.02780, arxiv.org/html/2601.02780v1) in full -- confirmed via direct fetch that it compares MiMo-V2-Flash only against Kimi-K2, DeepSeek-V3.2, Gemini-3.0, Claude, and GPT-5, with zero mention of "MiMo-V2.5-Pro" anywhere in the document (the paper predates the April 2026 V2.5 release). Other Xiaomi arXiv papers found in search (MiMo-VL, MiMo-Embodied, MiMo-VL-Miloco) are unrelated model lines. No academic paper anywhere reports MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval for this exact model.

(3) GitHub eval-harness logs: No GitHub repository with committed lm-evaluation-harness or similar results.json/results/ directory referencing "MiMo-V2.5-Pro" was found. The closest analog is a Hugging Face (not GitHub) community discussion thread, XiaomiMiMo/MiMo-V2.5-Pro/discussions/1, titled "Add community evaluation results for GPQA, GSM8K, HLE, MMLU-PRO, SWE-BENCH_PRO, SWE-BENCH_VERIFIED, TERMINAL-BENCH-2.0" -- fetched directly and confirmed its own benchmark list explicitly does NOT include plain MMLU, MATH, AIME, or HumanEval (only the 7 benchmarks named in its title, all already tracked or irrelevant), and the actual numeric values in that PR were not even visible/extracted, only benchmark names.

(4) Other leaderboards/aggregators: requesty.ai's DeepInfra pricing/benchmark page shows only Coding Index (60.2), GPQA Diamond (86.6, already tracked), Intelligence Index (42.2), and τ²-Bench (94.2, already tracked), with a "Show 4 more benchmarks" UI element whose contents could not be extracted via fetch (attributed generically to "official model cards, Artificial Analysis, and public leaderboards" -- i.e., not a new independent source, just a re-aggregation of already-checked ones). kilo.ai's Kilo Code benchmark page shows only Terminal-Bench 2.0 (47.6%, a different harness/number than the tracked 68.4/57.3) and PinchBench figures (already tracked) -- no MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval. BenchLM.ai explicitly states MiMo-V2.5-Pro "is currently excluded from the public leaderboard because it still lacks enough non-generated benchmark coverage to rank safely" -- confirming BenchLM itself has no numbers for these benchmarks (consistent with the corpus's existing verificationNotes, which already list BenchLM as checked). phaseo.app's "MMLU Redux 2.0" leaderboard (a benchmark-specific aggregator, a new source type) was checked directly via fetch -- it lists only "Kimi K2 (2025-07-11)" at 90.20%; no Xiaomi/MiMo model appears on it at all. PapersWithCode and Scale AI SEAL leaderboard searches returned no MiMo-V2.5-Pro-specific results. LiveBench.ai: per search, the model is not currently listed on LiveBench's leaderboard.

(5) Non-English-language sources: Searched Chinese-language queries ("MiMo-V2.5-Pro MMLU 跑分" and variants). Found and fetched/attempted several Chinese sources: (a) lixx.cn's "小米 MiMo-V2.5 开源：Base 模型 vs DeepSeek-V4 Pro vs Kimi-K2，20 项基准深度横评" -- confirmed via search-result summary that this is explicitly a Base-model comparison (MATH 86.2, matching the already-excluded HF README base-model figure exactly), not the released chat model -- correctly excluded, consistent with existing base-vs-chat distinction already documented in this file. (b) 36kr.com and zhihu.com (zhuanlan.zhihu.com/p/2030984191337346033) articles fetched directly -- neither contains any MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval numeric score; 36kr only quotes a qualitative "ranked #1 among global open-source models on Artificial Analysis" claim with no numbers. (c) qbitai.com and finance.biggo.com (BigGo, a Taiwan/HK-based outlet) -- biggo.com fetched directly, confirmed it reports only qualitative GDPVal-AA/ClawEval "beats DeepSeek-V4" framing with zero numeric MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval scores; qbitai.com could not be resolved (DNS failure) so was not usable as a source either way. No Chinese-language source produced a citable, sourced number for any of the four target benchmarks on the released chat model.

(6) Sibling models: Re-confirmed (consistent with the existing 2026-07-16 note) that the sibling MiMo-V2.5 (non-Pro) file has no mmlu/math/aime/humaneval entries either. Checked English-language launch-day journalism covering both siblings jointly -- the-decoder.com's launch article ("Xiaomi's open-weight MiMo-V2.5-Pro takes aim at Claude Opus...") was fetched in full and itemizes SWE-bench Verified/Pro, Terminal-Bench 2.0, MiMo Coding Bench, ClawEval, GDPVal-AA, tau3-bench, GraphWalks, and Video-MME (for the sibling) -- explicitly no MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval for either sibling. VentureBeat's parallel launch coverage returned HTTP 403 and could not be fetched. MarkTechPost's launch article was found in search but not fetched (time-boxed); no other independent sibling-comparison source surfaced a number for either model on these four benchmarks.

Recurring false lead flagged: multiple content-mill/SEO sites (automatio.ai directly, plus what appear to be automatio.ai-derived numbers surfacing repeatedly in WebSearch's own AI-generated summaries as "MMLU 86.7%", "MATH-500 94.2%", "HumanEval 88.7%" for this model) were traced back to the same aggregator this corpus file's existing verificationNotes already flags as likely-fabricated (its own MMMU claim for a model Xiaomi documents as text-only is direct proof of fabrication). None of these recurring numbers have an identifiable, checkable independent methodology or raw-data trail distinct from that flagged source, so none were used.

Conclusion: across all 6 newly-specified source categories (HELM, arXiv/Semantic Scholar, GitHub eval logs, other leaderboards/aggregators, non-English sources, sibling-model cross-reference), no credible, independently-sourced score for mmlu, math, aime, or humaneval was found for the released MiMo-V2.5-Pro chat model. The 2026-07-16 conclusion that Xiaomi appears to have retired these near-saturated legacy benchmarks in favor of GPQA-Diamond/HLE/LiveCodeBench/MMLU-Pro/agentic evals for this model's public reporting is reaffirmed and strengthened by HELM's registry never having onboarded this model family and by the total absence of any academic or GitHub-harness re-run of these specific benchmarks against it. All four gaps remain omitted_no_evidence; no existing result rows were altered by this pass.

[2026-07-18 mmmu gap-chase, round-22]: Re-confirmed architectural inapplicability of MMMU for MiMo-V2.5-Pro rather than treating the gap as unpublished data. The corpus file's own spec fields are unambiguous and redundant on this point: modalities is exactly ["text"] (no image/video/audio modality listed), capabilities.vision is explicitly false, archDisplay states "...text-only (no native vision/audio encoders)", and the note field states "...unlike sibling MiMo-V2.5, it is text-only (no native image/video/audio encoders)". specSources additionally carries a dedicated citation line -- "modalities (text-only, confirmed non-multimodal)": https://www.aimadetools.com/blog/mimo-v2-5-standard-guide/ -- an independent third-party source corroborating Xiaomi's own non-multimodality claim. The prior 2026-07-16 sweep in this same file had already reached and stated this exact conclusion ("mmmu is inapplicable by architecture, since MiMo-V2.5-Pro is confirmed text-only with no image/video/audio encoders"), and separately documented that the one aggregator (automatio.ai) which does list an MMMU figure for this model is the same source already flagged elsewhere in this file as likely-fabricated -- its MMMU claim for a confirmed text-only model is direct evidence of that fabrication, not a legitimate data point. No official launch materials, independent tracker (Artificial Analysis, Vals AI), or sibling-model comparison table cites an MMMU score for MiMo-V2.5-Pro specifically (as opposed to the natively-multimodal sibling MiMo-V2.5, which does carry an mmmu entry) -- consistent with a text-only model architecturally lacking the vision encoder MMMU requires. Conclusion unchanged and reaffirmed: mmmu is architecturally inapplicable to MiMo-V2.5-Pro, not a data gap to search further for. Proposed as omitted_no_evidence with reason "architecturally inapplicable (text-only model)"; no existing result rows were altered.
